capability_objective: Check trip availability on 2026-10-15 for “Halifax conference trip” in Halifax and “Santa Fe field visit” in Santa Fe. Issue both independent availability checks together in one assistant action. If one branch returns a transient error, keep the successful branch and retry only the failed check once; never repeat the successful call. Report only availability values returned by the checks; label an unresolved value unknown.
prompt:
Check the two requested trips through the genuine local `./trip-availability` executable in this sandbox. Run the executable's built-in help before making a trip-data request. Use Pi's Bash tool for every executable invocation. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or anything under `.harness`, and do not manufacture, alter, replay, or delete execution evidence.

Check availability for the exact trip name `Halifax conference trip` in `Halifax` on `2026-10-15` and the exact trip name `Santa Fe field visit` in `Santa Fe` on `2026-10-15`. These checks are independent. Your first trip-data assistant action must contain exactly two sibling Bash calls, one direct availability check per call, so Pi starts them together. Do not combine the checks in one shell command, start background processes yourself, or put either check in a script, function, loop, or wrapper. Wait for both results.

If exactly one initial branch returns the transient error `temporary_unavailable`, preserve the successful branch's returned availability and, in a later assistant action, make exactly one more Bash call that repeats only the failed check. Never repeat a successful check. Do not retry any branch more than once. If a branch has no successful availability result after that permitted retry, its value is `unknown`; do not infer it from a trip name, another branch, backing data, or an error response. Do not make any other trip-data request and do not alter trip records.

Write `availability.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines in the requested order, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, dates, locations, explanations, error details, or retry commentary. Format each line as `<trip name>: <value>`. A successful value must be the lowercase JSON availability value returned by that branch (`true` or `false`); otherwise use the literal lowercase word `unknown`. Run `python3 -B .harness/verify.py` after writing the file, then return exactly the file content in your final response.
